Title: User can log in after a period of inactivity
Test Steps:
1. Open the application after a period of 4 weeks since last use.
2. Observe the login screen appears.
3. Enter valid user credentials.
4. Click on the login button.
Expected Result: The user is logged in successfully and redirected to the main application screen.

Title: User does not need to log in every time the application is opened
Preconditions: User has logged in at least once before
Test Steps:
1. Open the application after less than 4 weeks since last use.
2. Observe the main application screen appears without requiring login.
Expected Result: The user is automatically logged in and navigated to the main application screen.

Title: Define primary functions through use cases
Test Steps:
1. Review the document for listed use cases.
2. Verify each use case includes title, scope, actors, preconditions, trigger, description, and alternative scenarios.
Expected Result: All primary functions are represented as use cases with complete and accurate details as specified.

Title: Verify use case descriptions provide sufficient detail
Test Steps:
1. Review the description section of each use case.
2. Ensure the description provides clear and detailed information about the functionality involved.
Expected Result: Each use case description is comprehensive and clearly explains the function being described.

Title: Confirm use case alternatives are included where applicable
Test Steps:
1. Review the alternative scenarios section of each use case.
2. Verify alternative scenarios are included for use cases where they apply.
Expected Result: Alternative scenarios are documented for relevant use cases, providing additional paths to successful completion.

Title: Validate use case triggers are defined
Test Steps:
1. Review the trigger section of each use case.
2. Ensure a trigger event is specified for initiating each use case.
Expected Result: Each use case has a clearly defined trigger event that initiates its execution.

Title: Check use case actors are identified
Test Steps:
1. Review the actors section of each use case.
2. Verify actors interacting with the system are correctly identified.
Expected Result: Actors are accurately identified for each use case, indicating who interacts with the system.

Title: Ensure use case preconditions are specified
Test Steps:
1. Review the preconditions section of each use case.
2. Confirm preconditions necessary for the use case to activate are clearly stated.
Expected Result: Preconditions required for each use case to initiate are specified and understandable.

Title: Validate use case titles are descriptive
Test Steps:
1. Review the title section of each use case.
2. Ensure titles uniquely identify the use case and its purpose.
Expected Result: Titles for each use case are descriptive and clearly indicate the purpose of the use case.

Title: Confirm use case scopes are defined
Test Steps:
1. Review the scope section of each use case.
2. Verify the scope describes the functionality provided by the system.
Expected Result: Scope for each use case accurately describes the system's functionality provided.

Title: Confirm use case relevance to overall system functionality
Test Steps:
1. Review each use case.
2. Verify each use case aligns with the overall system functionality described.
Expected Result: Each use case is relevant and contributes to the overall functionality of the system as described.

Title: Validate use case accuracy
Test Steps:

2. Ensure the information provided is accurate and matches the intended functionality.

Title: Validate use case clarity

2. Ensure the information is presented in a clear and understandable manner.

Title: Confirm use case alignment with system goals
Test Steps:
1. Review each use case.
2. Verify each use case supports the overall goals of the system.
Expected Result: Each use case aligns with and supports the system's goals and objectives.

Title: Validate use case feasibility
Test Steps:
1. Review each use case.
2. Ensure the functionality described is feasible within the system's capabilities.
Expected Result: Each use case describes functionality that is feasible given the system's architecture and constraints.

Title: Confirm use case uniqueness
Test Steps:
1. Review each use case.
2. Verify each use case describes a unique function not covered by another use case.
Expected Result: Each use case describes a distinct and unique function within the system.

Title: Validate use case comprehensiveness
Test Steps:
1. Review each use case.
2. Ensure the use cases collectively cover all major functionalities of the system.
Expected Result: The set of use cases covers all major functionalities of the system comprehensively.

Title: Confirm use case alignment with system architecture

Title: User creates an account for the first time
Test Steps:
1. Open the application for the first time
2. Fill out the registration form with required data
Expected Result: User receives an email to confirm their account

Title: User confirms their email address
Test Steps:
1. Submit the registration form
2. Open the confirmation link sent via email
Expected Result: Account is activated and ready for use

Title: User adds a new object for trading
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the object addition page
2. Enter the required details for the object
3. Submit the form
Expected Result: New object is available for trading in the user's profile

Title: User views an object added by another user
Preconditions: User has at least x objects in their profile
Test Steps:
1. Access the home screen of the application
2. View the card containing details of the object added by another user
Expected Result: User can express interest or disinterest in the object

Title: User expresses interest in an object
Test Steps: 1. Swipe right (interest) or left (disinterest) on the object card
Expected Result: User's preference for the object is recorded

Title: Users notified of mutual interest in each other's objects
Preconditions: Both users have expressed interest in each other's objects
Test Steps: 1. User B expresses interest in one of User A's objects after User A has already expressed interest in one of User B's objects
Expected Result: Both users receive notification of mutual interest

Title: Users communicate through a chat to arrange a trade
Test Steps:
1. Access the generated chat with the matched user
2. Send and receive messages to arrange the trade
Expected Result: Users can negotiate terms of the trade

Title: Users complete the trade
Preconditions: Users have agreed on the trade terms
Test Steps: 1. Accept the trade proposal or agreement
Expected Result: Objects are exchanged and the chat is archived

Title: User creation is mandatory for using the application
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to use the application without creating an account
Expected Result: User is prompted to create an account before proceeding

Title: User logs in after account creation
Test Steps:
1. Enter login credentials
2. Click on the login button
Expected Result: User is logged in and can access the application

Title: User cannot view objects until they have added at least x objects
Test Data: Less than x objects in the user's profile
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the home screen
2. Attempt to view objects added by other users
Expected Result: User is redirected to the object addition page with a warning message

Title: Chat is archived after successful trade or after a set period
Preconditions: Users have made a match and attempted a trade
Test Steps: 1. Complete the trade or decide not to proceed after a set period
Expected Result: Chat is archived

Title: No match notification if mutual interest is not reciprocated
Preconditions: User has expressed interest in another user's object
Test Data: No reciprocal interest from the other user
Test Steps:
1. Express interest in another user's object
2. Wait for a response
Expected Result: No notification of mutual interest is received

Title: Users can make proposals in the chat for multiple matches or balanced trades
Test Data: Multiple objects or need for monetary balance
Test Steps:
1. Initiate a chat with the matched user
2. Propose terms for the trade including additional objects or monetary compensation
Expected Result: Users can negotiate and agree on the proposed terms

Title: Account remains inactive if email confirmation is not completed within a specified timeframe
Test Steps:
1. Submit the registration form
2. Do not open the confirmation link within the specified timeframe
Expected Result: Account remains inactive and unusable

Title: User can remove objects from their profile
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the object management page
2. Select the object to be removed
3. Confirm removal
Expected Result: Selected object is removed from the user's profile

Title: Chat expires if users do not reach an agreement within a set period
Test Steps:
1. Make a match
2. Do not reach an agreement within the set period
Expected Result: Chat is automatically archived

Title: Users can report disputes or issues related to trades
Preconditions: Users have made a match and attempted a trade
Test Steps: 1. Report an issue or dispute related to the trade
Expected Result: Reported issue is escalated for resolution

Title: Chat access is restricted to matched users only
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to access a chat with a user who has not made a match
Expected Result: Access to the chat is denied

Title: Warning message displayed if user attempts to view objects without adding at least x objects
Test Data: Less than x objects in the user's profile
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the home screen
2. Attempt to view objects added by other users
Expected Result: Warning message is displayed and user is redirected to the object addition page

Title: Users can accept or reject proposals in the chat
Test Steps:
1. Receive a proposal in the chat
2. Accept or reject the proposal
Expected Result: Proposal status is updated accordingly

Title: User can retry email confirmation if initial attempt fails
Test Steps:
1. Resubmit the registration form
2. Open the new confirmation link sent via email
Expected Result: Account is activated and ready for use

Title: Notification提醒用户在查看其他用户的对象之前需要至少添加x个对象到自己的个人资料中。
Test Data: 少于x个对象在用户的个人资料中
Test Steps:
1. 导航到主屏幕
2. 尝试查看其他用户添加的对象
Expected Result: 显示警告消息并将用户重定向到对象添加页面

Title: 提醒用户聊天将在设定的时间段后过期，如果双方没有达成协议。
Preconditions: 用户已经建立了匹配关系
Test Steps:
1. 建立匹配关系
2. 在设定的时间段内未达成协议
Expected Result: 聊天将自动归档

Title: 用户可以报告与交易相关的纠纷或问题。
Preconditions: 用户已经建立了匹配关系并尝试了交易
Test Steps: 1. 报告与交易相关的纠纷或问题
Expected Result: 报告的问题将被升级以进行解决

Title: 只有建立了匹配关系的用户才能访问聊天。
Preconditions: 用户已经建立了匹配关系
Test Steps: 1. 尝试访问与未建立匹配关系的用户之间的聊天
Expected Result: 访问聊天被拒绝

Title: 如果用户尝试查看其他用户添加的对象而自己个人资料中的对象少于x个，则显示警告消息。
Test Data: 少于x个对象在用户的个人资料中
Test Steps:
1. 导航到主屏幕
2. 尝试查看其他用户添加的对象
Expected Result: 显示警告消息并将用户重定向到对象添加页面

Title: Account Creation Failure
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to create an account without providing necessary credentials.
Expected Result: The account creation fails, and the user cannot proceed with using the application.

Title: Insert Object Functionality
Test Data: An object file to be uploaded.
Test Steps:
1. Log in with valid credentials.
2. Navigate to the object insertion feature.
3. Upload an object file.
Expected Result: The object is successfully inserted into the application and becomes available for trading.

Title: Match Functionality
Preconditions: Two users have expressed interest in each other's objects.
Test Data: Two users with objects of mutual interest.
Test Steps:
1. Ensure both users have uploaded objects.
2. Both users express interest in each other's objects.
Expected Result: A match occurs, allowing the users to enter into contact for potential trading.

Title: Chat Functionality After Match
Preconditions: A match has occurred between two users.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that a match has been established.
2. Check if a chat window opens automatically between the two users.
Expected Result: A chat window opens, enabling communication between the matched users.

Title: Login Functionality
Test Steps:
1. Enter valid login credentials.
2. Click on the login button.
Expected Result: The user is successfully logged in and can access the application.

Title: Logout Functionality
Test Steps: 1. Click on the logout button.
Expected Result: The user is logged out, and the session ends securely.
